document.source_title: Algeria eliminates trachoma as a public health problem

claims.claim_text: Algeria has eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.
claims.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) has validated Algeria as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.

entities.name: Algeria
entities.description: Algeria has eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.
entities.evidence_text: Algeria’s elimination of trachoma is a historic triumph.

entities.name: World Health Organization
entities.description: The organization that validated Algeria's elimination of trachoma.
entities.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) has validated Algeria as having eliminated trachoma.

events.event: Submission of dossier for trachoma elimination validation
events.evidence_text: In December 2025, the Ministry of Health of Algeria compiled and submitted a dossier in which evidence was provided that the country meets the criteria set by WHO for elimination of trachoma.
WHO-compliant surveys conducted in 2022 confirmed that the elimination threshold for active trachoma was achieved in all areas.

metrics.name: Number of countries eliminating trachoma
metrics.evidence_text: making it the 10th country in WHO’s African Region and the 29th country globally to achieve this significant milestone.

metrics.name: Number of people at risk of trachoma blindness
metrics.evidence_text: According to latest figures, 97 million people live in trachoma endemic areas and are at risk of trachoma blindness.

citations.title: Algeria eliminates trachoma as a public health problem

source_credibility.reason: The information is provided by the World Health Organization.
